dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses context-dependent direction (long/short based on trend). Entry conditions use price crossing 10-period MA (sma_10) with confirmation of directional movement (price making higher/lower closes). Exit uses hard stop-loss (via ATR multiple) and trend reversal (price crossing back below/above MA). Trailing stop parameters captured in atr_multiple fields. 'Higher highs' trend confirmation approximated using price direction confirmation. High volume reversal condition difficult to express without volume weighting; basic MA cross used as primary reversal signal.

source_rule_text: Enter trades when the market direction shifts in your intended direction, then let profits run while cutting losses short.
